title: guajava pohliana
intro: Guajava pohliana, also known as Guajava, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant's leaves and bark possess medicinal properties, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Guajava pohliana include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and alleviating fever. Guajava pohliana is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it can also be used as a topical application to treat skin conditions and wounds.
